summary: PR #2320 matched or improved two miscellaneous functions across lb audio and mn name entry code: implemented lbAudioAx_80024DC4, implemented mnName_8023749C, updated a static audio pool allocation type, adjusted HSD object allocation calls to use the embedded alloc field, added language/name-list externs, and updated the mnName_8023749C header prototype.

key_files.role: Implemented lbAudioAx_80024DC4 and updated existing allocation/free/init call sites to pass &lbl_80433710.alloc after lbl_80433710 became an aggregate pool struct.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.c hunk replacing /// #lbAudioAx_80024DC4 with function body
raw_diff: HSD_ObjFree/HSD_ObjAlloc/HSD_ObjAllocInit call sites changed from &lbl_80433710 to &lbl_80433710.alloc
text_corpus: bot report lists lbAudioAx_80024DC4 as 100.00% new match

key_files.role: Replaced static HSD_ObjAllocData lbl_80433710 with lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc containing alloc, x2C, and x70 arrays.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.static.h hunk defining lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc size 0xB4
changed_files.jsonl: lbaudio_ax.static.h added 7 deleted 1

key_files.role: Implemented mnName_8023749C, added lblanguage include, added mnNameNew extern arrays, and used a scoped opt_common_subs pragma around the function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnname.c hunk adding #include "lb/lblanguage.h"
raw_diff: mnname.c hunk adding extern char* mnNameNew_803EE720[] and mnNameNew_803EE724[]
raw_diff: mnname.c hunk replacing /// #mnName_8023749C with function body
text_corpus: bot report lists mnName_8023749C as 97.84% after improvement

key_files.role: Updated mnName_8023749C prototype from unknown return/params to char* mnName_8023749C(int slot).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnname.h hunk changing UNK_RET mnName_8023749C(UNK_PARAMS) to char* mnName_8023749C(int slot)

what_changed: Implemented lbAudioAx_80024DC4 as two 0x10-iteration loops over lbl_80433710.x2C: first refreshing x70[i] to 0xa when arg0 is already present, then claiming the first slot whose value equals 0x83D60U.

Introduced lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc in lbaudio_ax.static.h with embedded HSD_ObjAllocData alloc at offset 0x00, x2C[17] at 0x2C, and x70[17] at 0x70, replacing lbl_80433710's previous plain HSD_ObjAllocData type.
Adjusted lbAudioAx_ObjFree, lbAudioAx_800263E8, and lbAudioAx_8002835C to pass &lbl_80433710.alloc to HSD object allocation APIs.

Implemented mnName_8023749C to select between mnNameNew_803EE724 and mnNameNew_803EE720 based on lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS(), scan until the requested slot or string terminator, and return NULL if the selected entry starts with mnName_StringTerminator.
Added #pragma push / #pragma opt_common_subs off / #pragma pop around mnName_8023749C.
Updated mnName_8023749C's public prototype to char* mnName_8023749C(int slot).
smart_moves: Recovered lbl_80433710 as a typed aggregate rather than continuing to treat the base symbol as only an HSD_ObjAllocData, allowing pool arrays to be accessed as fields while preserving object allocator call sites through the alloc subfield.
Kept lbAudioAx_80024DC4 as natural loops over the 16 active slots instead of repeated or manually unrolled slot checks.
Updated the owning header prototype for mnName_8023749C after recovering the function signature.
Scoped the opt_common_subs pragma with push/pop rather than leaving an unbounded pragma state change.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a static allocator symbol also owns nearby per-slot state, a small wrapper struct can expose the embedded HSD_ObjAllocData plus recovered arrays; existing HSD_ObjAlloc/HSD_ObjFree/HSD_ObjAllocInit call sites then need to pass the allocator subfield.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_80433710 changed from HSD_ObjAllocData to lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc
raw_diff: allocator call sites changed to &lbl_80433710.alloc

decomp_lessons.lesson: For fixed-size pool scans, natural for-loops over the slot count can express both duplicate refresh and empty-slot claim behavior cleanly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbAudioAx_80024DC4 uses two for loops with i < 0x10

decomp_lessons.lesson: When function recovery proves a signature, update the header prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in the same PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnName_8023749C body returns char* and takes int slot
raw_diff: mnname.h prototype updated to char* mnName_8023749C(int slot)

naming_conventions.name: lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc
naming_conventions.lesson: Struct name ties the recovered aggregate to the lbAudioAx pool allocation role while preserving unknown field names as offsets x2C and x70.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef struct lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc with fields alloc, x2C, x70

naming_conventions.name: alloc
naming_conventions.lesson: Use a semantic field name for the embedded HSD_ObjAllocData when allocator API call sites prove that role.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_ObjFree/HSD_ObjAlloc/HSD_ObjAllocInit use &lbl_80433710.alloc

naming_conventions.name: x2C, x70
naming_conventions.lesson: Keep offset-style field names when array roles are only partially recovered; the PR evidence shows usage but does not prove semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc fields x2C[17] and x70[17]
raw_diff: lbAudioAx_80024DC4 reads x2C and writes x70

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a typed aggregate around an existing data symbol to match field offsets and preserve allocator API typing.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.static.h defines lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc
raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.c uses st->x2C, st->x70, and &lbl_80433710.alloc
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The recovered arrays are named by offsets and the PR slice does not include independent data/symbol ownership evidence beyond the diff.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use scoped #pragma opt_common_subs off around mnName_8023749C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push, #pragma opt_common_subs off, function body, #pragma pop
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The bot report shows mnName_8023749C at 97.84%, not a confirmed 100% match, and the slice does not explain why the pragma is required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Cast slot to u8 and string terminator/entry bytes to s8 in the mnName_8023749C loop and terminator check.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for condition j != (u8) slot && term != (s8) array[j][0]
raw_diff: terminator comparison uses (s8) mnName_StringTerminator == (s8) str[0]
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The PR evidence shows the casts but does not include assembly notes explaining their necessity.

Verification evidence is mixed: the PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, but the loaded decomp-dev[bot] report lists lbAudioAx_80024DC4 as 100.00% and mnName_8023749C as 97.84%, plus .bss improvement.
No human review comments are available, so reviewer intent or acceptance rationale cannot be inferred.

The PR body's description of the name entry function as appending a string terminator does not directly match the shown implementation, which selects and returns a name-list entry or NULL; the diff is treated as stronger evidence for behavior.
The slice does not include build logs, objdiff output, or assembly, so matching tactics are recorded as observed code-shape tactics rather than proven requirements.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover embedded allocator aggregates when call sites prove the allocator subfield
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2320 shows lbl_80433710 changed from HSD_ObjAllocData to a lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc aggregate with HSD_ObjAllocData alloc at offset 0, while HSD_ObjFree/HSD_ObjAlloc/HSD_ObjAllocInit call sites were updated to use &lbl_80433710.alloc. Candidate lesson: when a data symbol combines allocator state with adjacent pool arrays, prefer a typed wrapper and use the embedded allocator field at API boundaries.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.static.h lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc definition
raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.c allocator API call-site updates

evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnname.h prototype update
raw_diff: mnname.c mnName_8023749C implementation

Curator may want to cross-link this case under the existing avoid-pragmas/register-asm or matching-tactics standards as an example of evidence that should be documented.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's claim that both functions reached fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 as an accepted validation result; the loaded bot report conflicts for mnName_8023749C.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no review comments were loaded.
Do not rename x2C, x70, or 0x83D60U semantically from this slice alone; the diff shows use but not enough meaning.
Do not treat the opt_common_subs pragma as generally acceptable style; this slice lacks proof that it was required and harmless.
